title: Can capital gains on stocks be shown as business income to avoid tax if total income is under ₹12 lakh?
summary: Reclassifying trading profits as business income may lower tax liabilities for some investors. This classification depends on factors such as trading frequency and intent. Taxpayers must consistently apply their chosen classification to avoid disputes with the Income Tax Department during audits.

title: Overtime rules in India: What the new labour codes say about your rights
summary: India's new labour codes consolidate 29 laws into four, establishing rules for working hours, overtime pay, and worker protections. Employers must pay double for overtime, and all workers, regardless of designation, are entitled to wage protections.&nbsp;

title: US Child Tax Credit: What to expect in 2026
summary: The Child Tax Credit provides a dollar-for-dollar reduction in tax liability for dependents under 17. The OBBB introduces a maximum credit of $2,200 per child for 2026, with unchanged income limits and specific IRS requirements for claiming.

title: Why US Social Security payout will be delayed in May 2026? When will the payments come?
summary: Social Security payments for May will be delayed due to the calendar, not policy changes. Payments will be issued on May 13, 20, and 27 based on recipients' birthdates.&nbsp;

title: Will Kevin Warsh cut rates as Fed chair? Should consumers anticipate immediate relief for mortgages, auto loans?

title: Are car lease and driver salary component in your CTC taxable? All you need to know
summary: Car leases and driver salaries can provide tax-efficient salary structures for employees. Tax regulations allow these perks to be taxed at lower values, according to experts.

title: Even in heavy debt, these savings remain protected from creditors | What the law says
summary: Loans can lead to debt, but Indian law protects certain savings like EPF, PPF, and NPS from creditors. These funds ensure long-term financial security during financial distress, safeguarding families from losing their assets.

title: HRA exemption rules: Income Tax Department explains how rent, salary, and city determine tax savings
summary: The Income Tax Department clarified that claiming HRA exemption does not guarantee significant tax savings. Benefits depend on rent paid, salary structure, and city type, with specific conditions for claiming exemptions. Details here.
